pr.title: fix(vertex): strip anthropic-beta header from countTokens requests
pr.body:
## Summary

Fixes #1016

When using the Vertex AI backend, `client.messages.countTokens()` and `client.beta.messages.countTokens()` forward the `anthropic-beta` header to Vertex's `count-tokens:rawPredict` endpoint. Vertex model backends roll out beta values on different schedules — the Haiku tier frequently lags — so any call that includes an unrecognized beta value receives an HTTP 400:

```
"Unexpected value(s) `effort-2025-11-24` for the `anthropic-beta` header."
```

Token counting does not require any beta-gated capabilities, so there is no reason to forward these headers. This PR strips `anthropic-beta` inside `buildRequest` at the same point where the path is rewritten to `count-tokens:rawPredict`.

## Changes

- **`packages/vertex-sdk/src/client.ts`**: In the `count_tokens` branch of `buildRequest`, apply `buildHeaders([options.headers, { 'anthropic-beta': null }])` to explicitly remove the `anthropic-beta` header before forwarding to Vertex. The `null` sentinel follows the existing convention used throughout the SDK to delete a header.
- **`packages/vertex-sdk/tests/client.test.ts`**: Add four new tests covering:
  - Beta header is stripped from non-beta `count_tokens` requests
  - Beta header is stripped from beta `count_tokens?beta=true` requests
  - Beta header is **not** stripped from regular `/v1/messages` requests
  - The path is correctly rewritten to `count-tokens:rawPredict`

## Test plan

- [x] All 12 tests in `packages/vertex-sdk/tests/client.test.ts` pass (`yarn test`)
- [x] No changes to generated SDK code
- [x] Fix is isolated to the Vertex `buildRequest` override and does not affect the Bedrock or main SDK paths
pr.author: xodn348
